qa_pairs.question: What modifications are required for the MATLAB Parallel Server Slurm plugin files after the initial installation?
qa_pairs.answer: After installing the MATLAB Parallel Server Slurm plugin, navigate to the `nonshared` folder within MATLAB's support package root. Then, in both `independentSubmitFcn.m` and `communicatingSubmitFcn.m`, replace the line defining `additionalSubmitArgs` with `ccSBATCH().getSubmitArgs();`. Finally, in the `communicatingJobWrapper.sh` file, add `module load matlab/2022a` (adjusting the MATLAB version to match your local installation) around line #20.

qa_pairs.question: How should users validate their MATLAB Parallel Server setup, and why is the built-in validation tool discouraged?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should avoid using the built-in validation tool within the Cluster Profile Manager. Instead, the recommended method is to use the `TestParfor` example available by downloading and extracting code samples from GitHub at `https://github.com/ComputeCanada/matlab-parallel-server-samples`. The built-in tool is not fully supported, and only its first two tests will function correctly.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find official documentation, self-paced online courses, and external resources for MATLAB?
qa_pairs.answer: For official MATLAB documentation, users can visit `https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/`. Self-paced online courses are available through MATLAB Academy at `https://matlabacademy.mathworks.com/`. Additionally, some universities, such as the University of Calgary, offer their own MATLAB documentation and job script examples at `https://rcs.ucalgary.ca/MATLAB`.
